      "raw_quote": "the master may ask him about past, present and future things, and he will at once give full response in writing."
    },

      "name": "Lilith (Lilit / Lylet)",
      "type": "spirit",
      "attributes": {"role": "Hebrew demoness", "appears with servants/knights"},
      "page_ref": "p. 129–130",
      "raw_quote": "The First Mirror of Lilith (spelled Lilit or Lylet) is dedicated to this ancient Hebrew demon and to her followers and her ‘knights’"
    },

      "name": "Bylet",
      "type": "spirit",
      "attributes": {"note": "text slips into calling the spirit Bylet"},
      "page_ref": "p. 130",
      "raw_quote": "although the text seems to slip into calling the spirit Bylet before the experiment is over"
    },
    {
      "name": "Clm 849 (Munich Handbook)",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"number_of_experiments": 19 divinatory, "focus": detection of crime"},
      "page_ref": "p. 126–134",
      "raw_quote": "The largest number of experiments in Clm 849, nineteen in all, is divinatory."
    },
    {
      "name": "Mirror of Floron (v1 & v2)",
      "type": "tool",
      "attributes": {"material": "pure steel", "diameter": one palm, "inscriptions": 10 names + spirit name},
      "page_ref": "p. 128–130",
      "raw_quote": "Have a mirror made of pure steel, measuring one palm around... inscribed with ten characters... Floron written in the middle"
    },
    {
      "name": "First Mirror of Lilith",
      "type": "tool",
      "attributes": {"form": "shield optional", "medium": mirror},
      "page_ref": "p. 129–130",
      "raw_quote": "The master commands these ‘demons’ to appear in non-threatening form and answer questions."
    },
    {
      "name": "polished thumbnail/fingernail scrying",
      "type": "tool",
      "attributes": {"medium": boy’s left thumb nail", "preparation": scraped with knife"},
      "page_ref": "p. 132–134",
      "raw_quote": "Five divinatory experiments in the Munich handbook... require the medium to gaze into his own polished fingernail or thumbnail."
    },
    {
      "name": "Egippia, Benoham, Beanke vel Beanre, Reranressym, Alredessym, Ebemidyrr, Fetolinie [?], Dysi, Medirini, Alhea, Heresim, Haham, Ezirohias, Bohodi, Hohada, Anna, Hohanna, Ohereo, Metaliteps, Aregereo, Agertho, Aliberri, Halba",
      "type": "spirit",
      "attributes": {"role": "names inscribed on slip for nail scrying", "used in conjuration"},
      "page_ref": "p. 133",
      "raw_quote": "bind to the same thumb... a slip with the following names written on it: Egippia, Benoham, Beanke vel Beanre..."
    },
    {
      "name": "virgin boy medium",
      "type": "person",
      "attributes": {"age": <12, "birth status": legitimate, "gender": male (mostly), "role": scrying medium"},
      "page_ref": "p. 127–134",
      "raw_quote": "the medium must be a virginal boy... sometimes younger than twelve, and born of legitimate marriage"
    },
    {
      "name": "sacrifice to spiritus aerialis (rooster)",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"context": Norfolk 1465 case", "victim": baptized rooster"},
      "page_ref": "p. 127",
      "raw_quote": "they promised it the body of a Christian man... baptized a rooster with a Christian name and sacrificed it to the spirit"

      "type": "spirit",
      "attributes": {"role": "aerial demon", "appears in treasure-finding context"},
      "page_ref": "p. 127",
      "raw_quote": "When a spiritus aerialis appeared, at Bunwell, they promised it the body of a Christian man..."
    },

      "type": "spirit",
      "attributes": {"role": "binding names used in Solomon’s bond"},
      "page_ref": "p. 133–134",
      "raw_quote": "by which Solomon bound demons in a glass vessel"

      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"type": "conjuration", "used to compel spirits"},
      "page_ref": "p. 132",
      "raw_quote": "if the spirit refuses, the master reads the unbreakable ‘bond of Solomon’, an extended conjuration"
    },

      "name": "Clm 849 experiment no. 27",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"focus": theft detection", "medium": fingernail scrying"},
      "page_ref": "p. 133–134",
      "raw_quote": "No. 27 is broken into two ‘chapters’, the first of which reads as follows: To find out about a theft, take a virgin boy..."
    },

      "name": "Haleytum, Gaptametuntij, Morto, Orfail, Geibel, Huabaton",

      "raw_quote": "...haleytum gaptametuntij morto orfail geibel huabaton albital hualepin..."
    },

      "name": "Clm 849 experiment no. 25",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"focus": serious purpose only", "prohibition": no base/frivolous use"},
      "page_ref": "p. 127",
      "raw_quote": "The text of one experiment (no. 25) insists that the procedure must not be used for any base or frivolous purpose, but only for a serious one"
    },

      "name": "Clm 849 experiment no. 24",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"medium": crystal", "spirits": angels", "preparation": washed with wine, names of Christ written in oil"},
      "page_ref": "p. 131–132",
      "raw_quote": "the master first takes a crystal and washes it with wine, then he writes certain names of Christ on it with olive oil"

      "name": "Clm 849 experiment no. 20",
      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"medium": polished steel mirror", "timing": Venus-Jupiter conjunction"},
      "page_ref": "p. 131",
      "raw_quote": "In one of the remaining experiments (no. 20) the master goes to a secret place at a conjunction of Venus and Jupiter..."
    },

      "type": "ritual",
      "attributes": {"medium": mirror", "preparation": olive oil, verbena-sprinkled circle"},
      "page_ref": "p. 132",
      "raw_quote": "In the other experiment (no. 33), the master smears olive oil on a mirror... enters a magic circle that is sprinkled with verbena"

  "summary": "This chunk details divinatory experiments from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focusing on scrying practices using mirrors, crystals, and fingernails, with young male mediums. It extracts named spirits like Floron, Lilith, and Bylet, lists ritual tools and preparations, and references historical cases involving aerial demons and treasure-finding. The text emphasizes the role of innocence (virginal boys), protective conjurations invoking divine names, and the frequent use of binding formulas derived from Solomon.",
  "key_passages": [
    "The largest number of experiments in Clm 849, nineteen in all, is divinatory... the usual end is detection of a crime or criminal (usually a thief)...",
    "Have a mirror made of pure steel... around the rim... have these ten names inscribed... with the name of the aforesaid spirit [Floron] written in the middle.",
    "The First Mirror of Lilith (spelled Lilit or Lylet) is dedicated to this ancient Hebrew demon and to her followers and her ‘knights’...",
    "Five divinatory experiments in the Munich handbook... require the medium to gaze into his own polished fingernail or thumbnail.",
    "To find out about a theft, take a virgin boy of legitimate birth... bind to the same thumb... beneath the nail, a slip with the following names written on it: Egippia, Benoham, Beanke vel Beanre..."
  ]
